msgProfileExists: A patient profile with the name "{name}" already exists!
msgConfirmDelete: Delete profile "{name}"? This will cascaded-delete all their historical training sessions!
msgCannotDeleteLast: Cannot delete the only remaining patient profile in the database!
msgConfirmClear: Completely purge all training history for this patient? This action is permanent!
lblRotationBlock: 📱 Please rotate your device to portrait mode
sessionTimerCompleted: Outstanding focus! Your timer has finished. Please look away at a distant object for 20 seconds to relax your ciliary muscles before continuing.
helpSessionLimit:
🏥 Purpose: Strictly limits daily visual workload, preventing cortical fatigue.
🎮 Difficulty: Depends on the selected trial limit (more trials = higher strain).
🧭 Recommendation: Standard limit: 80 trials for Gabor and 25 for stereograms.
helpTimerLimit:
🏥 Purpose: Automatically halts the session, prompting a rest break to prevent ocular muscle fatigue and accommodative spasm.
🎮 Difficulty: Helps control eye strain and continuous focus endurance.
🧭 Recommendation: Ophthalmologists recommend 10–15 minute sessions (the 20-20-20 rule).
descModeExplanation: Preset guidelines: Select an active protocol as a starting template. Modify any checkbox below to seamlessly transition into 'Custom' configuration mode.
helpPresetMode: Clinical pre-sets (active macros) designed for binocular, peripheral, or rapid temporal stimulation.
helpStartLevel:
🏥 Purpose: Sets starting spatial frequency. Higher stages have ultra-fine lines, challenging foveal photoreceptors and primary visual cortex (V1) networks.
🎮 Difficulty: Stage 1 — easiest (thick lines); Stage 5 — extreme (micro-acuity lines).
🧭 Recommendation: Start at Stage 1. Advance only when maintaining sustained accuracy >85%.
helpAutonext:
🏥 Purpose: Automatically triggers the next flash 1 second after your answer, maintaining continuous cortical rhythm and central fixation lock.
🎮 Difficulty: Streamlines rhythmic progression.
🧭 Recommendation: Keep ON to avoid extra clicks and maintain eye focus on the central cross.
helpFlashDuration:
🏥 Purpose: Blocks compensatory eye movements (saccades). Ultra-short flashes force instant processing, preventing the strong eye from 'cheating' by shifting its gaze.
🎮 Difficulty: 350ms — easy; 100ms — extremely fast (blitz mode).
🧭 Recommendation: Use 'Adaptive' (duration automatically tightens as accuracy increases).
helpStageAdvance:
🏥 Purpose: Allows the algorithm to automatically raise or lower stages based on a rolling accuracy window of the last 15–20 trials.
🎮 Difficulty: Keeps the exercise on the edge of your active sensory capabilities.
🧭 Recommendation: Keep ON to maintain an optimal neuroplastic load.
helpStatic:
🏥 Purpose: The Gabor patch stays visible indefinitely until an answer is selected.
🎮 Difficulty: Easiest mode (no temporal limit or speed pressure).
🧭 Recommendation: Use for initial training, familiarization, or when teaching small children.
helpFlicker:
🏥 Purpose: Pulsates the target Gabor at 10 Hz (Alpha-resonance), overriding top-down cortical suppression and preventing Troxler's fading.
🎮 Difficulty: Requires high focus but significantly aids low-contrast detection.
🧭 Recommendation: Highly effective for stubborn, deep binocular suppression.
helpPermanentCross:
🏥 Purpose: Retains a faded central cross during the flash, assisting in central fixation and countering eccentric gaze drift.
🎮 Difficulty: Makes central fixation significantly easier.
🧭 Recommendation: Recommended for patients with strabismus or nystagmus.
helpLowContrast:
🏥 Purpose: Allows the contrast staircase to descend down to the extreme 1% threshold instead of stopping at the standard 5%.
🎮 Difficulty: Extremely high. Lines become almost invisible to an untrained eye.
🧭 Recommendation: Enable only after mastering Stage 5 at standard contrast.
helpWideVariance:
🏥 Purpose: Randomizes line density and size with every flash, preventing neural frequency adaptation in the visual cortex.
🎮 Difficulty: Slightly increases task diversity.
🧭 Recommendation: Keep ON to stimulate a broader population of V1 cortical receptive fields.
helpShapeVariance:
🏥 Purpose: Randomly squashes the Gabor patch into an ellipse, challenging orientation selectivity and countering retinal astigmatism.
🎮 Difficulty: Increases orientation ambiguity on the edge of detection.
🧭 Recommendation: Recommended for patients with complex astigmatism.
helpPeripheral:
🏥 Purpose: Randomly shifts the Gabor patch, training parafoveal awareness, spatial coordination, and peripheral attention.
🎮 Difficulty: High. Requires resolving line tilts using peripheral vision without looking away from the center cross.
🧭 Recommendation: Enable in later stages of rehabilitation to restore spatial localization.
helpCrowding:
🏥 Purpose: Surrounds the target Gabor with flanking distractors, training the brain to separate detail under active binocular noise and countering visual crowding.
🎮 Difficulty: High. Central and flanking lines tend to blur together.
🧭 Recommendation: Highly recommended for school-age children struggling with crowded text while reading.
helpCrowdingMode:
🏥 Purpose: Directs lateral inhibition across specific retinal meridians.
🎮 Difficulty: Vertical — standard; All sides — maximum spatial noise density.
🧭 Recommendation: Use vertical axis to train vertical paragraph-tracking for reading.
helpFlankerDistance:
🏥 Purpose: Spacing at 2x sigma challenges spatial crowding. Spacing at 4x sigma acts as a collinear neural amplifier, helping to resolve line tilt at ultra-low contrast thresholds.
🎮 Difficulty: 2x spacing — hard; 4x spacing — easier (assists with low-contrast recognition).
🧭 Recommendation: Use 4x spacing for ultra-low contrast training.
helpOrthogonal:
🏥 Purpose: Rotates flanking bars perpendicular to the target, creating distinct orientation boundaries.
🎮 Difficulty: Eases target localization due to the contrasting angle differences.
🧭 Recommendation: Use in early stages of lateral crowding training.
helpDynamic:
🏥 Purpose: Runs a continuous phase wave inside the flankers, saturating the strong eye's motion channels to help the weak eye isolate the target.
🎮 Difficulty: Temporarily reduces dominant eye suppression.
🧭 Recommendation: Recommended for patients with strong visual suppression.
lblSynopStatusAlign: Look strictly at the center dot! Use peripheral vision and drag the screen (or WASD keys/tap canvas edges) to align the targets.
lblSynopStatusTraining: Fusion locked! Muscles are slowly pulling the eye back to center... Maintain alignment!
synopBreakText: Great attempt! You held the binocular fusion down to {current} px offset (started at {start} px). Your extraocular muscles successfully compensated for {percent}% of the strabismus deviation! With daily practice, maintaining focus will become effortless. Take a short break.
synopSuccessText: Outstanding achievement! You have fully pulled your eyes' visual axes to the geometric center (0 pixels) without losing fusion. Your binocular vergence has completely compensated for the deviation. Rest for 10 seconds and repeat to consolidate the muscle memory!
synopWarnAlign: Please shift the target first to match your deviation!
helpSynopPullSpeed:
🏥 Purpose: Determines the automatic target pulling speed, exercising vergence reserves.
🎮 Difficulty: Slower (4.0s) — easy; Faster (1.5s) — high muscle load.
🧭 Recommendation: Start at 4.0s or 2.5s to allow gentle muscle adaptation.
helpTargetType:
🏥 Purpose: Selects the central sensory anchors for binocular fusion.
🎮 Difficulty: 'Ring & Dot' — easy (forgiving); 'Cross & Square' — hard (requires strict X/Y alignment).
🧭 Recommendation: Start with Ring, advance to Cross as deviation angles reduce.
helpTargetSize:
🏥 Purpose: Determines the retinal stimulation area. Foveal sizes require microscopic fusion precision.
🎮 Difficulty: Macular (65px) — easy; Foveal (30px) — requires extreme gaze stability.
🧭 Recommendation: Advance to Foveal size to secure lasting orthoptic alignment on micro-deviations.
helpSynopLazyGrid:
🏥 Purpose: Projects dashed guidelines to the weak eye's target, helping the brain locate its spatial position.
🎮 Difficulty: Substantially eases initial target capture.
🧭 Recommendation: Enable for larger squint angles to speed up fusion.
helpSynopStrongGrid:
🏥 Purpose: Projects dashed guidelines to the strong eye's static center, stabilizing central gaze.
🎮 Difficulty: Aids in locking the dominant eye's fixation.
🧭 Recommendation: Best used in combination with the Lazy Eye Grid.
helpSynopFlicker:
🏥 Purpose: Pulsates the weak eye's target at 10 Hz, suppressing binocular suppression and aiding fusion.
🎮 Difficulty: Assists in holding fusion locks when the weak eye tends to fade.
🧭 Recommendation: Highly recommended if the target regularly disappears in glasses.
helpSynopLockY:
🏥 Purpose: Locks vertical deviation to zero (Y=0), isolating horizontal training.
🎮 Difficulty: Prevents vertical finger drag errors.
🧭 Recommendation: Ideal for standard horizontal esotropia/exotropia.
helpSynopLockX:
🏥 Purpose: Locks horizontal deviation to zero (X=0), isolating vertical training.
🎮 Difficulty: Prevents horizontal finger drag errors.
🧭 Recommendation: Ideal for standard vertical hypertropia/hypotropia.
helpRdsStartDisparity:
🏥 Purpose: Sets initial target shift. Lower values train fine sensory connections in V2 (micro-stereopsis).
🎮 Difficulty: 8px — easy, thick relief; 1px — fine micro-3D on the edge of depth perception.
🧭 Recommendation: Start at 6-8px. Aim to master figure recognition at 1px.
helpRdsDotSize:
🏥 Purpose: Adjusts individual dot scale. Smaller dots yield denser correlation grids, aiding 3D depth pop-out.
🎮 Difficulty: 2px — easy to fuse but requires high optical clarity; 6px — requires greater fusion effort.
🧭 Recommendation: Use 4px or 2px dot scale for optimal balance.
helpRdsDensity:
🏥 Purpose: Adjusts filled dot percentages. 50% density provides optimal binocular contrast matching.
🎮 Difficulty: 50% is easiest; deviations in either direction increase fusion difficulty.
🧭 Recommendation: Keep ON standard 50%.
helpRdsAutonext:
🏥 Purpose: Automatically triggers next trial 1 second after answer, keeping active cortical pace.
🎮 Difficulty: Streamlines continuous 3D training.
🧭 Recommendation: Keep ON to increase density and focus.
helpRdsPermanentCross:
🏥 Purpose: Projects a central zero-disparity cross to stabilize gaze and assist initial stereoscopic fusion.
🎮 Difficulty: Eases visual focus alignment.
🧭 Recommendation: Keep ON to assist initial 3D pop-out.
helpRdsDynamic:
🏥 Purpose: Shuffles random noise at 18Hz to completely block monocular blinking cheats.
🎮 Difficulty: Enables monocular cues, forcing stereoscopic integration.
🧭 Recommendation: Keep ON to guarantee authentic binocular training.
helpRdsRandomizeVertical:
🏥 Purpose: Randomizes the vertical offset of the target, expanding spatial localization training.
🎮 Difficulty: Moderately increases scanning difficulty.
🧭 Recommendation: Enable once foveal stereo-acuity stabilizes.
helpRdsFloating:
🏥 Purpose: Adds a slow, smooth drifting motion to the hidden square, forcing dynamic vergence tracking to build extraocular muscle stamina.
🎮 Difficulty: Highly challenging; requires continuous muscle tracking.
🧭 Recommendation: Enable for dynamic muscle training.
helpRdsFloatSpeed:
🏥 Purpose: Adjusts the drifting speed of the floating 3D square.
🎮 Difficulty: Slow — gentle drift; Fast — intensive vergence chase.
🧭 Recommendation: Start on Slow speed to build tracking stamina.
helpLang:
🏥 Purpose: Configures interface language and optimizes typographical layouts for native readability.
🎮 Difficulty: Does not affect exercises.
🧭 Recommendation: Set your preferred language for the most intuitive guidance.
helpAnaglyph:
🏥 Purpose: Splits color channels for dichoptic training, routing separate imagery to each eye simultaneously.
🎮 Difficulty: Requires Red-Cyan 3D glasses and balanced calibration.
🧭 Recommendation: Core protocol for active binocular suppression training.
helpStrongAttenuation:
🏥 Purpose: Attenuates contrast for the dominant eye, countering active visual suppression of the weaker eye.
🎮 Difficulty: 10% — easiest (strong eye heavily dimmed); 100% — hardest (equal contest).
🧭 Recommendation: Start at 20-30%, gradually advancing to 100% as the weak eye recovers.
helpFusionLock:
🏥 Purpose: Renders visual stabilization frames visible to both eyes to prevent ocular drift during rapid exposures.
🎮 Difficulty: Greatly stabilizes binocular focus.
🧭 Recommendation: Keep ON across all dichoptic (3D) exercises.
msgImportConfirm: Are you sure you want to import {count} training sessions into the active profile "{name}"?
msgImportSuccess: Successfully imported {count} training sessions into the active profile!
msgImportError: Failed to parse CSV file. Please make sure the format is valid and untouched.
chartPlaceholder: Complete at least 2 sessions to plot your contrast sensitivity trend line.
chartLegend: 📈 Progress Trend: movement of the curve upward represents clinical improvement in cortical sensitivity (threshold reduction).
synopChartLegend: 📈 Vergence Progress: movement of the curve upward represents clinical alignment toward 0.0Δ. Last 10 sessions.
rdsChartLegend: 📈 Stereopsis Progress: movement of the curve downward represents clinical improvement in stereoscopic resolution (disparity threshold reduction). Last 10 sessions.
csvHeaders: Date,Mode,Lazy Eye Side,Score/Total,Accuracy %,Gabor Stage,Contrast Threshold %,Contrast Balancer %,10Hz Flicker,Visual Crowding,Peripheral Shift,Permanent Cross,Synop Deviation X (px),Synop Deviation Y (px),Synop Start Distance (px),Outcome,RDS Dot Size (px),RDS Dot Density %,RDS Disparity Threshold (px)
infoAboutText: Amblyopia is a neurological impairment of the primary visual cortex (V1), where the brain actively suppresses signals from the weaker eye. Gabor patches and Random Dot Stereograms correspond mathematically to the receptive fields of V1/V2 neurons. Forcing the visual cortex to decode line orientation and 3D disparity at the absolute contrast threshold breaks suppression and triggers synaptic remodeling (Hebbian plasticity).
infoGaborText:
• Preparation: Patch the dominant (strong) eye completely. Always wear your prescribed optical correction (glasses/contacts) for distance.
  • Positioning: Hold the device at a stable distance of 50–70 cm.
  • Process: Gaze strictly at the central cross (+). Resolve line tilts using peripheral attention during the ultra-short flash (100–350 ms), which physically prevents the eye from shifting its gaze (saccadic cheats).
  • Progression: The automatic 1-up 3-down contrast staircase reduces line contrast to an extreme 1% as your accuracy improves, locking in high-frequency foveal resolution.
infoSynopText:
• Purpose: Treating strabismus (latent eye drift of 10–15 pixels) and strengthening extraocular muscles.
  • Sensory Fusion: Drag targets across the screen (or use WASD keys) to align the dot perfectly inside the ring at your subjective squint angle. This relaxes and calms spasm-prone muscles.
  • Motor Vergence: Press 'LOCK FUSION'. The software will slowly pull targets back to true center (0,0). Maintain fusion, forcing the muscles to contract, expanding your vergence reserves and correcting the eye drift.
infoRdsText:
• Preparation: Do not patch either eye. Wear calibrated Red-Cyan 3D glasses with both eyes open.
  • Calibration: Open the L/R test and use the W/S keys to balance the strong eye's contrast, bypassing the weak eye's suppression gate.
  • Dynamic Noise (DRDS): Boiling static at 18Hz completely blocks monocular blinking cheats, forcing V1/V2 stereoscopic neurons to integrate depth. Without glasses, the 3D shape is 100% invisible due to subpixel additive color blending on the retina.
  • Floating Target (Pursuit): A slow, smooth DVD-like bounce forces the extraocular muscles to track the target in 3D, training dynamic vergence.
  • Medals: Pushing disparity down to 1px (Stage 5) represents the absolute limit of micro-stereopsis (20 arcseconds), permanently locking in binocularity and preventing strabismus relapse.
infoSafetyText:
• Pacing: Perform 40–80 attempts daily. Rest is vital for memory consolidation; overtraining causes accommodative spasm.
  • Medals: 🥈 Silver Medal is awarded for completing your daily session limit (preventing fatigue). 🥇 Gold Medal represents absolute mastery of Stage 5 at micro-disparity.
  • Listen to Your Body: Stop immediately if your eyes water or feel painful. Prioritize comfort.
sessionCompleted: Session complete! You completed {limit} attempts. Take a short break to rest your eyes.
sessionMastered: Incredible focus! Your visual cortex has reached its maximum plastic saturation for today. You have successfully mastered Stage 5 at ultra-low contrast! Stop here to lock in these results and let your eyes rest. Outstanding job!
sessionCompletedRDS: Stereogram session complete! You completed {limit} attempts. Rest your eyes.
sessionMasteredRDS: Incredible! You have reached the absolute limit of micro-stereopsis at 1 pixel disparity. Stop here to lock in these results and let your eyes rest. Outstanding job!
